response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile diverges significantly from this role's core requirements. His professional C++ exposure is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; his systems-level strengths lie in DLang, Node.js, and TypeScript. He shows no evidence of MQTT, industrial automation protocols, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or Azure, and lacks the sustained C++/STL, multi-threaded networking depth this position demands. His high-throughput, event-driven, and mentoring experience is genuine but transfers only partially.
strengths: Deep experience with high-performance, low-latency, event-driven systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and a track record of mentoring and running workshops
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, desktop, web, and geo-data platforms, including Kubernetes deployment
concerns: Minimal recent professional C++ experience — the core requirement of the role — with only a brief C/C++ role in 2014–2015
No demonstrated experience with industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL processes, or automation protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus)
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, suggesting misalignment with an industrial C++ architecture role
